articles.title: भीषण गर्मी और लू का कहर! भोपाल में 30 अप्रैल तक स्कूल बंद, इन जिलों में भी छुट्टी की घोषणा, जानें अपडेट - Dailyhunt
articles.search_term: लू ("Agar Malwa" OR Alirajpur OR Anuppur OR Ashoknagar OR Balaghat OR Barwani OR Betul OR Bhind OR Bhopal OR Burhanpur OR Chhatarpur OR Chhindwara OR Damoh OR Datia OR Dewas OR Dhar OR Dindori OR Guna OR Gwalior OR Harda OR Hoshangabad OR Indore OR Jabalpur OR Jhabua OR Katni OR Khandwa OR Khargone OR Mandla OR Mandsaur OR Morena OR Narsinghpur OR Neemuch OR Panna OR Raisen OR Rajgarh OR Ratlam OR Rewa OR Sagar OR Satna OR Sehore OR Seoni OR Shahdol OR Shajapur OR Sheopur OR Shivpuri OR Sidhi OR Singrauli OR Tikamgarh OR Ujjain OR Umaria OR Vidisha) Madhya Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Bhopal School Closed: मध्य प्रदेश में अप्रैल के महीने में ही सूरज आग उगल रहा है। प्रदेश के ज्यादातर जिलों में गर्मी और भयंकर लू का अलर्ट है। चिलचिलाती धूप और लू चलने की वजह से बच्चों को स्कूल आने-जाने में मुश्किल हो रही है।
ऐसे में भीषण गर्मी को देखते हुए मध्य प्रदेश के कई जिलों में बच्चों की छुट्टी की घोषणा की गई है।
भोपाल के सभी स्कूलों में कक्षा आठवीं तक के छात्रों के लिए 30 अप्रैल तक छुट्टी घोषित की गई है। जिलाधिकारी ने छुट्टी को लेकर आदेश जारी किया है। भोपाल के अलावा राज्य के कई अन्य जिलों में भी छुट्टी का ऐलान किया गया है, तो कहीं स्कूलों के समय में बदलाव किया गया है।
Bhopal School Closed: भोपाल के स्कूलों में 30 अप्रैल तक छुट्टी
Bhopal School Closed (Source: Social media)राजधानी भोपाल में नर्सरी से लेकर 8वीं तक के बच्चों के लिए 30 अप्रैल तक सभी स्कूलों में छुट्टी की घोषणा की गई है। यह फैसला भीषण गर्मी को देखते हुए लिया गया है, जो कि सरकारी के साथ-साथ प्राइवेट स्कूलों पर भी लागू होगा। हालांकि इस दौरान शिक्षकों को स्कूल में उपस्थित रहने के निर्देश दिए गए हैं। राज्य में पिछले कुछ दिनों से तापमान में हुई अचानक बढ़ोतरी और हीट वेव को देखते हुए लिया गया है। प्रशासन का मानना है कि इस भीषण गर्मी में बच्चों को स्कूल भेजना उसके स्वास्थ्य जोखिम भरा हो सकता है।
उज्जैन में बदला स्कूलों का समय
उज्जैन में भी झुलसाने वाली गर्मी पड़ रही है, जिसे देखते हुए नर्सरी से लेकर कक्षा 8 तक के सभी स्कूलों में छुट्टी की घोषणा की है। आदेश के अनुसार, उज्जैन में अब सरकारी और प्राइवेट स्कूलों में 27 अप्रैल से लेकर 30 अप्रैल तक छुट्टी रहेगी। इसके अलावा कक्षा 9 से लेकर 12वीं तक के लिए स्कूलों के समय में बदलाव किया गया है। अब इनकी क्लास सुबह 7:30 बजे से शुरू होकर 12:30 तक चलेंगी।
Bhopal School Closed News: ग्वालियर में भी छुट्टी का ऐलान
Bhopal School Closed News (Source: Social media)गर्मी को देखते हुए ग्वालियर में कलेक्टर ने 30 अप्रैल तक कक्षा 8वीं तक के सभी सरकारी और निजी स्कूलों में छुट्टी घोषित की है। वहीं, कक्षा 9वीं से 12वीं तक के स्कूल सुबह 7:30 बजे से दोपहर 12 बजे तक चलेंगे।

articles.title: Schools shut up to Class 8 in Bhopal: Holiday in Indore, Gwalior and Ujjain too; heatwave alert in 22 MP di... - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: heatwave ("Agar Malwa" OR Alirajpur OR Anuppur OR Ashoknagar OR Balaghat OR Barwani OR Betul OR Bhind OR Bhopal OR Burhanpur OR Chhatarpur OR Chhindwara OR Damoh OR Datia OR Dewas OR Dhar OR Dindori OR Guna OR Gwalior OR Harda OR Hoshangabad OR Indore OR Jabalpur OR Jhabua OR Katni OR Khandwa OR Khargone OR Mandla OR Mandsaur OR Morena OR Narsinghpur OR Neemuch OR Panna OR Raisen OR Rajgarh OR Ratlam OR Rewa OR Sagar OR Satna OR Sehore OR Seoni OR Shahdol OR Shajapur OR Sheopur OR Shivpuri OR Sidhi OR Singrauli OR Tikamgarh OR Ujjain OR Umaria OR Vidisha) Madhya Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Schools shut up to Class 8 in Bhopal:Holiday in Indore, Gwalior and Ujjain too; heatwave alert in 22 MP districts, rain likely in 3 days
- Copy link
Madhya Pradesh is facing intense heat, with temperatures rising sharply across many regions. Several cities recorded temperatures above 44°C, and a heatwave alert has been issued in 22 districts for Sunday.
Khajuraho was the hottest in the state, recording 44.6°C. Many other cities, including Ratlam, Datia, Dhar and Tikamgarh, also saw temperatures above 43°C. In major cities, Gwalior and Ujjain touched 43°C, while Bhopal recorded 42.4°C and Indore 42.2°C.
Heatwave alert in 22 districts
The heatwave warning covers districts such as Gwalior, Bhind, Datia, Tikamgarh, Chhatarpur, Satna, Mandla, Balaghat, Chhindwara, Raisen, Neemuch, Mandsaur, Ratlam, Dhar and Alirajpur. The impact of heat will continue in Bhopal, Indore and Ujjain, where temperatures are expected to stay above 41°C.
Rain, thunder likely from April 27
The Meteorological Department has forecast a change in weather from April 27. Thunderstorms, lightning and rain are likely in parts of the state for three days. Rain is expected in Gwalior, Chambal, Jabalpur and Sagar divisions on April 28 and 29 due to a western disturbance.
Schools shut, timings changed
Due to the rising heat, holidays have been declared for Anganwadi centres and schools up to Class 8 in Indore from April 27 to 30. Classes for senior students will run in the morning hours. Similar steps have been taken in Gwalior and Ujjain, while some schools in Bhopal have also announced holidays.
Demand for coolers and ACs rises
With temperatures soaring, demand for air conditioners and coolers has increased by 20–30% in Bhopal. Traders say prices have risen slightly, but new energy-efficient models are available in the market.
Weather turning unstable
Despite the heat, some areas are seeing sudden weather changes. Mandsaur received rainfall on Saturday. According to the weather department, cyclonic circulations and a trough are active, causing changes in weather conditions.
Advisory for people
The weather department has advised people to stay hydrated, avoid direct sunlight during afternoon hours, and wear light cotton clothes. Extra care should be taken for children and the elderly.
April heat at peak
Experts say April and May are the hottest months in the state, and temperatures usually peak during this period. In recent years, cities like Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur and Gwalior have regularly recorded temperatures above 43–45°C in April.
Overall, the state will continue to face severe heat for the next few days, with some relief expected later this week due to rain and thunderstorms.

articles.title: Severe April Heatwave Sparks El Nino Concerns, Temperature Crosses 43°C in Bhopal, Indore - Dailyhunt
articles.search_term: heatwave ("Agar Malwa" OR Alirajpur OR Anuppur OR Ashoknagar OR Balaghat OR Barwani OR Betul OR Bhind OR Bhopal OR Burhanpur OR Chhatarpur OR Chhindwara OR Damoh OR Datia OR Dewas OR Dhar OR Dindori OR Guna OR Gwalior OR Harda OR Hoshangabad OR Indore OR Jabalpur OR Jhabua OR Katni OR Khandwa OR Khargone OR Mandla OR Mandsaur OR Morena OR Narsinghpur OR Neemuch OR Panna OR Raisen OR Rajgarh OR Ratlam OR Rewa OR Sagar OR Satna OR Sehore OR Seoni OR Shahdol OR Shajapur OR Sheopur OR Shivpuri OR Sidhi OR Singrauli OR Tikamgarh OR Ujjain OR Umaria OR Vidisha) Madhya Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Severe heat has gripped large parts of India in April, with Madhya Pradesh among the worst affected. Temperatures have surged past 43-45°C in several cities, disrupting daily life and triggering widespread heatwave alerts.
In the state capital Bhopal, the mercury touched 43°C on Sunday. Other cities also recorded extreme temperatures, including Indore at 43.2°C, Khajuraho at 45°C, Mandla at 44.9°C, Nowgong at 44.6°C, and Narmadapuram and Sagar at 44.4°C each. At least seven cities in the state have reported temperatures above 44°C.
The India Meteorological Department has issued heatwave alerts for 24 districts. Major cities including Bhopal, Indore, Gwalior, and Jabalpur remain under continuous heatwave conditions. Authorities have warned that temperatures may rise further over the next two days.
The intense heat has severely affected daily life, with deserted roads during peak afternoon hours as people avoid stepping out unless necessary.
The India Meteorological Department has issued heatwave alerts for 24 districts. Major cities including Bhopal, Indore, Gwalior, and Jabalpur remain under continuous heatwave conditions. Authorities have warned that temperatures may rise further over the next two days.
The intense heat has severely affected daily life, with deserted roads during peak afternoon hours as people avoid stepping out unless necessary.
El Nino Threat Behind Rising Heat
A key factor behind the extreme temperatures is the potential onset of El Nino. The World Meteorological Organization has warned that El Nino conditions may develop between May and July, earlier than previous estimates that placed it in the later monsoon phase (August-September).
El Nno is a recurring climate pattern that occurs every 2 to 7 years and typically lasts 9 to 12 months. It is caused by warming sea surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific Ocean, which disrupts global weather patterns and often leads to higher global temperatures.
India is likely to feel its impact significantly. The IMD has already forecast below-normal rainfall this year, estimating the monsoon at around 92% of the long-term average. Since nearly 70% of India's annual rainfall depends on the monsoon, any shortfall could have serious consequences.
Reduced rainfall may lead to lower crop production, increased water scarcity, and rising costs for essential goods. Agriculture and water resources are expected to be particularly vulnerable if the monsoon weakens.
According to the WMO, rising sea surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific indicate a growing likelihood of El Nino forming soon. During May-July, land temperatures are expected to remain above normal across many regions.
Typically, El Nino conditions bring reduced rainfall or drought-like situations in parts of India, Australia, and Indonesia, while increasing rainfall in regions such as South America, southern parts of the United States, parts of Africa, and Central Asia.
